sql_array: CREATE TABLE celestial_data (id INT PRIMARY KEY, declination DOUBLE, radius DOUBLE, description VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO celestial_data (id, declination, radius, description) VALUES (1, 5.0, 1.2, 'Star A'), (2, NULL, 2.5, 'Star B'), (3, 3.0, 0.0, 'Star C');
CREATE TABLE calculations (id INT PRIMARY KEY, alpha_value DOUBLE);
INSERT INTO calculations (id, alpha_value) VALUES (1, sys.alpha(declination, radius)) FROM celestial_data;
SELECT id, sys.alpha(declination, radius) AS expanded_alpha, ~COUNT(*) FROM celestial_data GROUP BY id HAVING expanded_alpha IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, ~sys.alpha(COALESCE(declination, 0.0), NULLIF(radius, 0.0)) FROM celestial_data WHERE declination BETWEEN -90 AND 90;
SELECT id, sys.alpha(~(declination + 1), radius) AS alpha_result FROM celestial_data JOIN calculations ON calculations.id = celestial_data.id WHERE calculations.alpha_value IS NOT NULL;
WITH alpha_calc AS (SELECT id, sys.alpha(~declination, ~radius) AS nested_alpha FROM celestial_data) SELECT id, nested_alpha FROM alpha_calc WHERE nested_alpha < 10.0;
CREATE TEMPORARY TABLE temp_results AS SELECT id, ~sys.alpha(declination, radius) AS altered_alpha FROM celestial_data WHERE declination IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, altered_alpha FROM temp_results WHERE altered_alpha < 0.5 UNION SELECT id, -1 * sys.alpha(declination, radius) FROM celestial_data WHERE radius = 0.0;
SELECT ~sys.alpha(declination, radius) AS negated_alpha FROM celestial_data ORDER BY negated_alpha DESC;
